title: Mastery Exam: CSS Motion Systems
summary: Mastery Exam: CSS Motion Systems retrieves prior skills in an original independent motion purpose timeline performance and debug decisions scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css transitions, css keyframes, css animation timing, css animation performance, css reduced motion, css animation debug in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real independent motion purpose timeline performance and debug decisions artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Ethical Product Launch motion and feedback work here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the Ethical Product Launch motion and feedback work here. */

steps.why: Mastery Exam: CSS Motion Systems makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in independent motion purpose timeline performance and debug decisions.

content.title: independent motion purpose timeline performance and debug decisions evidence
content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: CSS Motion Systems checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: CSS Motion Systems checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: CSS Motion Systems checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent motion purpose timeline performance and debug decisions case 1: transition all is harmless because browsers automatically choose only cheap useful properties.

Independent motion purpose timeline performance and debug decisions case 1: name the exact state change and properties, keep both endpoints understandable, and verify interruption, keyboard, preference, and performance behavior.
Independent motion purpose timeline performance and debug decisions case 1: animate width height margin color and every future property to make state changes feel consistent.

options.text: Independent motion purpose timeline performance and debug decisions case 4: transform and opacity are always free and need no performance verification.

Independent motion purpose timeline performance and debug decisions case 4: prefer transform or opacity when suitable, measure actual layout paint compositing and memory behavior, and remove speculative promotion hints.
Independent motion purpose timeline performance and debug decisions case 4: replace a measured cheap color change with a large composited layer because compositing is always faster.

options.text: Independent motion purpose timeline performance and debug decisions case 28: transform and opacity are always free and need no performance verification.

Independent motion purpose timeline performance and debug decisions case 28: prefer transform or opacity when suitable, measure actual layout paint compositing and memory behavior, and remove speculative promotion hints.
Independent motion purpose timeline performance and debug decisions case 28: replace a measured cheap color change with a large composited layer because compositing is always faster.

options.text: Independent motion purpose timeline performance and debug decisions case 29: a very fast animation automatically satisfies reduced-motion needs.

Independent motion purpose timeline performance and debug decisions case 29: remove nonessential spatial movement while preserving status and task feedback through immediate nonmotion cues and user control.
Independent motion purpose timeline performance and debug decisions case 29: hide the save status in reduced-motion mode because its animation cannot run.

options.text: Independent motion purpose timeline performance and debug decisions case 30: an animation transform always combines automatically with a transform declared elsewhere.
